title: Your First Notebook
description: Create your first NotebookLM notebook, add a website source, and explore the auto-generated summary.

steps.instruction: Open NotebookLM at notebooklm.google.com in a new tab.
Click the '+ Create new' button in the top-right corner to start a new notebook.
steps.coachText: Look for the '+ Create new' button at the top-right of the page.

steps.instruction: In the modal that appears, click 'Websites' to add a URL as your first source.
steps.coachText: Choose 'Websites' from the source options. You can also upload files, use Google Drive, or paste text.

steps.instruction: Paste any website URL into the input field (e.g., a Wikipedia article) and click 'Insert'.
steps.coachText: Paste any URL you like. Try a Wikipedia article to see how NotebookLM processes web content.

steps.instruction: Wait for NotebookLM to process your source and read the auto-generated Notebook Summary in the Chat panel.
steps.coachText: NotebookLM automatically generates a summary of your source with bolded key terms and inline citations.

title: Asking Grounded Questions
description: Learn how to ask questions in NotebookLM and understand inline citations that link back to your sources.

steps.instruction: Open NotebookLM and navigate to an existing notebook (or create one with at least one source).
Click one of the suggested follow-up questions below the summary, or click the 'Start typing...' chat box at the bottom.
steps.coachText: You can click suggested follow-up questions or type your own in the chat box.

steps.instruction: Type a question about your source material and press Enter to send it.
steps.coachText: Ask something specific about your source. NotebookLM will only answer based on the content you provided.

steps.instruction: Read the response and notice the numbered citation markers (1, 2, 3...) that reference specific passages in your source.
steps.coachText: Each numbered marker links to a specific passage in your source, ensuring every answer is grounded in your data.

steps.instruction: Click on one of the citation numbers to see the exact passage in your source that supports the answer.
steps.coachText: Clicking a citation shows you the exact text from your source that the AI used to generate its answer.

title: Saving Notes from Chat
description: Convert AI chat responses into permanent notes you can review later.

steps.instruction: Open a NotebookLM notebook that has at least one chat response visible.
Find a useful chat response and click the 'Save to note' button below it.
steps.coachText: The 'Save to note' button appears below each AI response, next to the copy and thumbs up/down buttons.

steps.instruction: You can also create a manual note by clicking the 'Add note' button in the bottom-right corner of the page.
steps.coachText: Manual notes let you add your own observations alongside AI-generated content.

title: Adding Multiple Sources
description: Upload a PDF and a website, then toggle sources on/off to control which content the AI uses.

steps.instruction: Open an existing NotebookLM notebook or create a new one.
In the left Sources panel, click '+ Add sources' to add another source.
steps.coachText: You can add up to 50 sources per notebook from different formats.

steps.instruction: Click 'Upload files' and upload a PDF document from your computer.
steps.coachText: NotebookLM supports PDF, images, docs, and audio files.

steps.instruction: Wait for the new source to be processed and appear in your source list.
Now toggle a source's checkbox OFF to exclude it from the AI's context, then ask a question. Notice how the AI only uses the remaining selected sources.
steps.coachText: Toggling sources lets you control exactly which documents the AI references when answering your questions.

title: Generating Flashcards & Quizzes
description: Use the Studio panel to generate study flashcards and self-assessment quizzes from your sources.

steps.instruction: Open a NotebookLM notebook that has at least one source added.
Look at the right-hand Studio panel and click 'Flashcards' to generate study cards from your sources.
steps.coachText: The Studio is on the right side of the screen. Flashcards will be auto-generated from your source material.

steps.instruction: Review the generated flashcards. Each card has a question on one side and an answer sourced from your documents.
steps.coachText: Flip through the flashcards to study key concepts from your uploaded content.

steps.instruction: Now go back to the Studio panel and click 'Quiz' to generate a self-assessment quiz.
steps.coachText: The Quiz tool creates multiple-choice or short-answer questions to test your knowledge.

steps.instruction: Answer a few quiz questions to test your understanding of the source material.
steps.coachText: Try answering the questions. The quiz is grounded in your source material.

title: Creating a Mind Map
description: Use the Studio to generate a visual mind map that shows concept hierarchies from your sources.

steps.instruction: Open a NotebookLM notebook with at least one source.
In the right-hand Studio panel, click the 'Mind Map' button.
steps.coachText: The Mind Map tool creates a visual hierarchy of themes and concepts from your sources.

steps.instruction: Wait for the mind map to be generated and explore the visual layout of concepts.
steps.coachText: The mind map visually shows how concepts in your source relate to each other.

steps.instruction: Click on different nodes in the mind map to explore sub-concepts and related themes.
steps.coachText: Try clicking on nodes to see their connections and drill deeper into topics.

title: Audio Overview Deep Dive
description: Generate an AI podcast-style audio discussion and customize the format, length, and focus area.

steps.instruction: Open a NotebookLM notebook with at least one rich source.
In the Studio panel, find 'Audio Overview' and click the Edit/Customize pencil icon next to it.
steps.coachText: Click the pencil ✏️ icon next to Audio Overview to access customization options before generating.

steps.instruction: Explore the format options: Deep Dive (conversation), Brief, Critique, or Debate. Select one.
steps.coachText: Each format produces a different style: Deep Dive is a friendly conversation, Critique adds analysis, Debate shows multiple perspectives.

steps.instruction: Set the length (Short, Default, or Long) and optionally add an Episode Focus to guide the discussion.
steps.coachText: The Episode Focus lets you direct the podcast to emphasize specific topics from your sources.

steps.instruction: Click the 'Generate' button to create your audio overview. This may take several minutes.
steps.coachText: Audio generation takes a few minutes. You can continue using the notebook while it processes.

steps.instruction: Once generated, play the audio to hear the AI-hosted podcast discussion about your source material.
steps.coachText: Listen to the AI hosts discuss your source material in a natural, engaging conversation.

title: Building a Slide Deck
description: Generate a professional presentation from your sources using the Studio's Slide Deck tool (BETA).

steps.instruction: Open a NotebookLM notebook with at least one source.
In the Studio panel, click the Edit/Customize pencil icon next to 'Slide Deck'.
steps.coachText: Slide Deck is marked as BETA. Click the pencil icon to customize before generating.

steps.instruction: Choose between 'Detailed Deck' (full text slides) or 'Presenter Slides' (visual cues only).
steps.coachText: 'Detailed Deck' includes full text, while 'Presenter Slides' gives visual cues for live presentations.

steps.instruction: Click 'Generate' and wait for NotebookLM to create your slide deck.
Review the generated slides. You can download or share them directly.
steps.coachText: Review each slide and note how NotebookLM organized your source content into a presentation structure.

title: Creating Infographics
description: Design visual infographics from your source data using the Studio's Infographic tool (BETA).

steps.instruction: Open a NotebookLM notebook with at least one data-rich source.
In the Studio panel, click the Edit/Customize pencil icon next to 'Infographic'.
steps.coachText: Infographic is a BETA feature. Click the pencil icon to set orientation, detail level, and style.

steps.instruction: Choose your preferred orientation (Landscape, Portrait, or Square) and adjust the level of detail.
steps.coachText: Pick an orientation that fits how you plan to use the infographic — Landscape for presentations, Portrait for print.

steps.instruction: Click 'Generate' and wait for the infographic to be created.
Review the generated infographic visual. You can download or share it.
steps.coachText: The infographic visually represents the key data points and concepts from your sources.

title: Multi-Source Research Synthesis
description: Combine multiple sources from different formats, use Fast Research, and generate a comprehensive Report.

steps.instruction: Create a new notebook or open one. Ensure you start with at least 2–3 different sources.
In the Sources panel, locate the web search bar and toggle on 'Fast Research' mode.
steps.coachText: Fast Research lets NotebookLM search the web and automatically add relevant sources to your notebook.

steps.instruction: Search for a topic in the web search bar to let NotebookLM find and add new sources automatically.
steps.coachText: Type any research topic. Fast Research will find and add relevant web sources to your notebook.

steps.instruction: With multiple sources loaded, go to the Studio panel and click 'Reports' to generate a structured report.
steps.coachText: The Reports tool synthesizes all your selected sources into a structured written summary.

steps.instruction: Review the generated report. Notice how it synthesizes information from multiple sources with citations.
steps.coachText: The report will cross-reference your sources, highlighting agreements and differences between them.

steps.instruction: Save the report as a note for later reference by clicking 'Save to note'.
steps.coachText: Saving the report as a note makes it permanently available in your notebook.
